thesis: Externally managed commercial mortgage REIT with a clean loan book (66% weighted average loan-to-value, zero realized losses, no central-business-district office exposure) trading at ~0.58 times book on sector-wide credit fear and a one-quarter earnings dip from unredeployed rights-offering capital.
why_market_wrong: The market prices sector CRE stress and temporary post-raise earnings dilution together, despite filing evidence of no realized losses, conservative underwriting, and ~$100M of cash plus unfunded commitments still to deploy at ~7.8% yields.

key_assumptions.source: Q1 2026 DE $0.24/sh × 4; temporary dilution from December 2025 rights offering
Book $14.90 − fee drag $1.50 − CRE reserve $1.50; evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-21.md

open_questions: Confirm redeployment of rights-offering capital back toward $0.28/quarter distributable earnings over the next two to three quarters.
Review whether sector price-to-book discount persists if realized losses stay at zero.
No approved third-party CRE sector source in base IRR; revisit after third-party scan picks up coverage.
Human review of thin synthesis path (16.04%) versus Lawrence scenario base (14.6%).
